entities.label: 6.15 “Make it stack”
entities.note: The 'one stack to financial parity' figure. Often quoted as 6.15 BTC.

entities.label: El Salvador
entities.sourceName: Bitcoin Treasuries (mirrors El Salvador National Bitcoin Office)
entities.note: Active daily DCA accumulation (~1 BTC/day) via the National Bitcoin Office under President Bukele. Continues post-IMF deal.

entities.sourceName: Laszlo Hanyecz — 22 May 2010, first commercial BTC transaction (2 Papa John’s pizzas)
entities.note: 10,000 BTC paid for two pizzas on 22 May 2010 — the first real-world BTC transaction. Now celebrated annually as Bitcoin Pizza Day.

entities.label: SpaceX
entities.note: Privately held; figure derived from on-chain address attribution rather than corporate disclosure. Tesla sister-company under Musk; no public buying programme.

entities.label: US Government
entities.note: Primarily from seizures (Silk Road, Bitfinex hack, etc.). Estimates range 198k–328k depending on whether seized-but-pending vs. fully forfeited assets are counted; this is the inclusive figure. Strategic Bitcoin Reserve being formalised; no active buying program.

entities.label: BlackRock IBIT
entities.note: Largest spot Bitcoin ETF globally by AUM. Subject to daily inflows/outflows.

entities.label: Strategy
entities.note: Formerly MicroStrategy. Largest corporate Bitcoin holder. Average cost basis ~$75,500/BTC. Continues weekly purchases.

entities.label: Satoshi
entities.note: Estimated from Patoshi-pattern address analysis. Dormant since 2010; widely accepted estimate but not 100% confirmed.

entities.label: Total supply
entities.note: Theoretical maximum supply at the protocol limit.
